name: Understanding the Robustness of Distributed Self-Supervised Learning Against Non-IID Data
content: Recent research has introduced distributed self-supervised learning (D-SSL) approaches to leverage vast amounts of unlabeled decentralized data. However, D-SSL faces the critical challenge of data heterogeneity, and there is limited theoretical understanding of how different D-SSL frameworks respond to this challenge. To fill this gap, we present a rigorous theoretical analysis of the robustness of D-SSL frameworks under non-IID (non-independent and identically distributed) settings. Our results
summary: 本文通过理论分析，揭示了分布式自我监督学习在非独立同分布数据下的鲁棒性问题，并提出了MAR损失的概念以提升其性能。
key_insights: Masked Image Modeling（MIM）的预训练方法比对比学习（CL）更具有对抗异质数据的能力。
联邦学习（FL）与分布式自我监督学习在平均网络连接度下的鲁棒性相当，优于传统的集中式学习。
MAR损失作为一种改进的MIM目标，通过局部到全局的对齐正则化来增强模型性能。
xihe_relevance: 对曦和的潜在价值在于提供了一种新的方法来提升分布式自我监督学习在非独立同分布数据下的鲁棒性。
